title: Remote Motion Designer Jobs in Canada | Montreal Jobs
description: Find remote and hybrid motion designer jobs in Canada. Real job listings from studios, agencies, and tech companies with CAD salary ranges and requirements.

mainEntity.name: How much does a remote motion designer make in Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: Salaries for remote motion designers in Canada typically range from $60,000 to $85,000 CAD for mid-level roles, while senior motion designers and art directors can earn between $90,000 and $120,000+ CAD. Rates vary heavily based on whether you work for a local agency or a US-based tech company hiring remotely in Canada.

mainEntity.name: What software do I need to know for motion design jobs?

acceptedAnswer.text: After Effects is the non-negotiable industry standard for 2D motion design. For 3D roles, Cinema 4D is highly preferred, often alongside Redshift or Octane renderers. Knowledge of Premiere Pro, Illustrator, and Photoshop is expected. Figma is becoming increasingly important for product-focused motion design.

mainEntity.name: Are there many fully remote motion design jobs in Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, motion design is highly conducive to remote work. While many traditional advertising agencies have moved to hybrid models (2-3 days in office), tech companies, gaming studios, and specialized production houses frequently offer fully remote roles for candidates based anywhere in Canada.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a formal degree to become a motion designer?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. In motion design, your showreel is your resume. While a degree in Graphic Design, Animation, or Film can be helpful for foundational knowledge, employers overwhelmingly prioritize the quality of your portfolio and your technical execution over formal education.
